channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump doubles planned tariffs on Canadian steel and aluminum to 50% as trade war intensifies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he will double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada, escalating a trade war with the United States' northern neighbor and showing an indifference to recent stock market turmoil and rising recession risks.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 4 charged in death of 5-year-old boy 'incinerated' in hyperbaric chamber explosion
item.description: Four people have been charged in the death of a 5-year-old boy who was “incinerated” inside a pressurized oxygen chamber that exploded at a suburban Detroit medical facility, Michigan's attorney general said Tuesday. Thomas Cooper from Royal Oak, Michigan, was pronounced dead at the scene on Jan.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why you'll no longer see 'excessive heat warnings' in the forecast
item.description: It may be hard to think about sweltering temperatures in early March but excessive heat watches and warnings are getting a new name.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Trump immigration crackdown enters new waters with arrest of Mahmoud Khalil
item.description: The arrest of a Columbia University graduate marks a significant escalation in President Trump's immigration crackdown, with a lawful permanent resident who has not been charged with any crime now being held in U.S. custody.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police in the Dominican Republic say FBI joins search for US student who vanished on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The FBI has joined the search for a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student who went missing nearly a week ago while on spring break in the Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street's sell-off worsens after Trump hikes tariffs
item.description: The U.S. stock market is sinking further Tuesday after President Donald Trump raised the stakes in his trade war, pulling Wall Street more than 9% below its record set just a month ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York fires more than 2,000 prison guards for refusing to return to work after illegal strike
item.description: More than 2,000 prison guards were fired on Monday for not returning to work after a weekslong strike that violated a state law prohibiting strikes by most public employees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American college student missing in Dominican Republic didn't drown, Natalee Holloway private eye believes
item.description: University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki's disappearance from a Punta Cana resort should be seen as suspicious, according to a private investigator.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the Menendez brothers' bid for freedom
item.description: The district attorney of Los Angeles County says he does not support resentencing for Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about the Menendez brothers' bid for freedom
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The district attorney of Los Angeles says he does not support the resentencing of Lyle and Erik Menendez, brothers who have spent more than 30 years in prison for killing their parents at their Beverly Hills home in 1989.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing American college student Sudiksha Konanki: Police reveal male friend is under investigation
item.description: The last person to see missing University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki alive has reportedly spoken out as authorities reveal a male friend is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: 3 killed after medical helicopter crashes in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died after a medical helicopter crashed near the Natchez Trace in Madison County, Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing American college student’s spring break disappearance ‘too early’ to rule drowning: Virginia sheriff
item.description: The hometown Virginia sheriff of missing University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki said that it is "too early" to rule her disappearance as a drowning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Para climber reaches new heights after injury, wants others to join her
item.description: "The battle's not against my competitors, like the battle is about how well I do," said Nat Vorel, a paraclimber.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Ogle/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Man finds money in Tennessee park, hands it over to police
item.description: When asked why he didn't keep the cash, Ryan Soules said, "Well, not my money, simply, but I didn't earn it. Somebody else did.
item.dc:creator: Andy Cordan, NewsNation

item.title: Menendez brothers: Los Angeles DA Nathan Hochman asks to withdraw predecessor's motion to free killers
item.description: Los Angeles District Attorney Nathan Hochman is seeking to withdraw his predecessor's motion to seek reduced sentences for the Menendez brothers, serving life.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Convicted Pentagon leaker Jack Teixeira faces court-martial while already serving 15-year sentence
item.description: Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, convicted of leaking Pentagon documents on the Ukraine war, faces court-martial Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Measles case confirmed in Maryland resident who recently travelled abroad
item.description: Early symptoms include a fever higher than 101 degrees, a runny nose, cough and red, watery eyes. A person will experience a red rash one to four days after early symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio says purge of USAID programs complete, with 83% of agency's programs gone
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is signaling that the Trump administration has finished its six-week purge of programs of the six-decade-old U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know ahead of the talks between the US and Ukraine in Saudi Arabia
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Saudi Arabia is to host talks on Tuesday between the United States and Ukraine in a new diplomatic push after an argument erupted during President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's Feb. 28 visit to the White House.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Christopher Bell holds off Denny Hamlin at Phoenix to win third straight NASCAR Cup Series race
item.description: Bell survived a late drag race with Hamlin to become the first NASCAR Cup Series driver to win three straight races in the NextGen car, edging his teammate by 0.049 seconds to win the second-closest race in Phoenix Raceway history on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A single-engine plane crashes near a Pennsylvania airport and all 5 aboard are taken to hospitals
item.description: A single-engine airplane carrying five people crashed and burst into flames Sunday in the parking lot of a retirement community near a small airport in suburban Pennsylvania, and everyone on board survived, officials and witnesses said. The fiery crash happened around 3 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina man called for end of death penalty before execution in final statement
item.description: "I want my closing statement to be one of love and a calling to my fellow Christians to help us end the death penalty," Brad Kieth Sigmon wrote ahead of his execution.
item.dc:creator: Kelci O'Donnell, WGN 9

item.title: Why these 2 states didn't 'spring forward' to daylight saving time this morning
item.description: You may have been groggy this morning thanks to our clocks “springing forward” an hour overnight. Unless, of course, you live in either of the two states that don’t observe daylight saving time.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Fire risk remains high on Long Island after brush fires
item.description: The fire danger remains high on Long Island as officials work to quell wind-driven brush fires that broke out Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WGN 9

item.title: Oldest former MLB player, who once replaced Mickey Mantle on Yankees' roster, dies
item.description: Art Schallock got his shot in the major leagues after a struggling Mickey Mantle was optioned by New York in 1951.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New York governor declares state of emergency for wind-driven brush fire on Long Island
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fast-moving brush fires fanned by high winds burned through a large swath of land on New York's Long Island on Saturday, spewing thick gray smoke into the sky and prompting the evacuation of a military base and the closure of a major highway. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Daytona 500 winner William Byron claims NASCAR Cup pole
item.description: On Saturday, Daytona 500 winner William Byron claimed the Busch Light pole during the 312-mile race at Phoenix Raceway. According to NASCAR, it's the 14th pole of the 27 year-old's career.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy released after Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Xavier Worthy was released Saturday after a district attorney in Texas declined to pursue charges against the Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver following an arrest in which he had been accused of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey police officer killed, second officer wounded in shooting by 14-year-old, authorities say
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — A 14-year-old shot and killed a police officer and wounded another Friday evening in New Jersey, authorities said, in a chaotic scene that witnesses described as seeing officers running down a busy street before they heard a volley of a dozen or more gunshots.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chick-fil-A introduces new spring menu items, brings back fan-favorite sandwich
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the arrival of spring with new seasonal menu items, including a returning fan favorite.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: South Carolina man executed by firing squad is first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: A South Carolina man convicted of murder was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner to die by that method in 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Louisiana student died after being punched in hazing ritual, police say
item.description: Baton Rouge police confirmed Southern University student Caleb Wilson died after being punched in the chest multiple times during a hazing ritual. One arrest has been made, and more suspects are expected.
item.dc:creator: Keymonte Avery, WGN 9

item.title: Trump offers pathway to citizenship for South African farmers, families
item.description: President Trump has offered an expedited pathway to U.S. citizenship to South African farmers, calling their treatment in the country "terrible" and citing property laws that impose "unjust racial discrimination" against white Afrikaner farmers.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9
